description: Chebyshev coefficients for optimal 1D Schrodinger potentials V_N(x) on [0,10] matching first N Riemann zeta zeros
parameterization: V(x) = sum_{k=0}^{K-1} c_k * T_k(2x/L - 1), Chebyshev polynomials
conclusion: V_N(x) does NOT converge on a fixed interval [0,10]. The Weyl law forces eigenvalue growth ~ n^2 on bounded domains, incompatible with zeta zero growth ~ n/log(n).
